content.problemStatement:
Codechef rounds are held on every Wednesday now, and not on any other days.

You are wondering whether there is a Codechef round today. You know it is the NthN^{th}Nth day of the week (Sunday is 111st, Monday is 222nd, Tuesday is 333rd, Wednesday is 444th and so on).

Can you tell whether there is a Codechef round today?


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single integer NNN, the day of the week.


Output Format
Output `YES` if there is a Codechef round today, else `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 7) representing the day of the week (1‑Sunday, 2‑Monday, ..., 7‑Saturday).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing "YES" if N corresponds to Wednesday (i.e., N = 4), otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: N=1 (Sunday) → NO. Second test case: N=4 (Wednesday) → YES.
N=7 (Saturday) → NO, N=3 (Tuesday) → NO, N=4 (Wednesday) → YES.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. For each test case read N. Since CodeChef rounds are held only on Wednesday, check if N equals 4. If true print "YES", else print "NO". This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
